question: Match the following MPLS router roles to their primary label operation.
An MPLS egress router performs two lookups to process a packet. What is the correct order of these lookups?
options: IP forwarding lookup, then Label lookup
Label lookup, then IP forwarding lookup
MAC address lookup, then Label lookup
correct_answers: Label lookup, then IP forwarding lookup

question: Which field in the IP header of an RSVP PATH message alerts intermediate routers to pass the message to the control plane?
Which protocols contribute information specifically used to populate the LFIB (Label Forwarding Information Base)?
The definition of a Forwarding Equivalence Class (FEC) includes which of the following aspects?
options: Forwarded with the same forwarding treatment

question: Match the networking technology to the location where FEC lookup is performed.
options: At every router along the path
Only at the iLER (Ingress Label Edge Router)

correct_answers.A: At every router along the path
correct_answers.B: Only at the iLER (Ingress Label Edge Router)

question: Which protocols can be used specifically for the distribution of transport labels?
In an MPLS VPN with Traffic Engineering, which labels are distributed using the 'Downstream on Demand' method?
How many Layer 2 headers typically appear in a PDU within an LSR for an MPLS L3 VPN service running over Ethernet?
What is the key property of OAM commands (like lsp-trace) regarding the return path of responses?
options: Responses return via the same LSP path
Responses are routed back via the IGP
Responses return via a secondary LSP
Responses are dropped at the eLER
correct_answers: Responses are routed back via the IGP

question: Which parameter acts as a switch to influence whether TTL values are propagated from the IP header to the MPLS label stack?
For a Layer 2 service, what is the effect on the customer IP TTL value when operating in Uniform mode compared to Pipe mode?
options: Uniform mode decrements customer TTL at every hop
Pipe mode hides intermediate hops from customer TTL
There is no difference; customer TTL remains unchanged in both modes for L2 services
Uniform mode resets customer TTL to 255
correct_answers: There is no difference; customer TTL remains unchanged in both modes for L2 services

question: In a networking context, what is referred to by the term 'Triple Play'?
Why would an egress router request Penultimate Hop Popping (PHP)?
options: To increase the security of the label stack
To avoid a performance penalty of doing both a label lookup and an IP routing lookup
To force traffic onto a secondary LSP
correct_answers: To avoid a performance penalty of doing both a label lookup and an IP routing lookup

question: When an RSVP session timeout occurs at an intermediate router (LSR), which messages are sent to tear down the LSP?
Which pair of RSVP optimizations provides protection against lost messages in busy networks?
Which Special Use label is sent by an ultimate router to request PHP (Penultimate Hop Popping)?
In Nokia SR OS, what are the default preference values used to ensure RSVP and LDP tunnels are preferred over regular IP routing?
How does an intermediate router distinguish between a regular IP packet and MPLS data plane traffic?
options: By examining the Ethertype field (e.g., 0x8847 for MPLS)
By looking at the Source IP address
By the size of the PDU
correct_answers: By examining the Ethertype field (e.g., 0x8847 for MPLS)

question: Match the Nokia SR OS command to its purpose in route redistribution.
options: Activate or export the policy into a protocol

correct_answers.B: Activate or export the policy into a protocol
